arts.ttl: Arsenal line-ups vs Man City as Calafiori and Lewis-Skelly split, Nwaneri starts
arts.sum: Canton’s Arsenal XI: Raya; Timber, Saliba, Gabriel, Lewis-Skelly; Partey, Rice, Odegaard, Nwaneri, Havertz, Martinelli. Wilmot's Arsenal XI vs Man City: Raya; Timber, Saliba, Gabriel, Calafiori; Rice, Partey, Odegaard; Nwaneri, Havertz, Martinelli.

arts.ttl: Samir Nasri's four-word comment about Arsene Wenger after leaving Arsenal summed him up
arts.sum: Samir Nasri's relationship with Arsenal fans quickly became toxic when he left to join rivals Manchester City, but he showed a different side by defending Arsene Wenger even after his departure

arts.ttl: Sesko and Tel in, Jesus among three sold - Arsenal alternative transfer window
arts.sum: As things stand, Arsenal having any new arrivals following Monday, February 3 is unlikely but an alternate transfer window could have seen up to five moves sanctioned. The 19-year-old looks set to remain in Germany, but again due to injuries, minutes would have most definitely been coming his way had he made the switch to north London.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's five worst January transfers of all time
arts.sum: Another loan spell followed, this time at Bolton Wanderers, before Wellington was sold back to Fluminense in 2016, having never made a senior appearance for Arsenal. However, Helder only scored one goal in 49 appearances for the Gunners and he was loaned out to Benfica in late 1996, before joining NAC Breda on a permanent deal the following year.

arts.ttl: Arsenal learn Watkins injury update amid transfer call as Cunha contract agreed
arts.sum: With all that being said, football.london has taken a look at the latest Arsenal January transfer news and rumours. With deadline day for the January window approaching, Mikel Arteta will need to work quickly in order to get new signings into Emirates Stadium.
